top_signals.title: Kuwait Is Set to Sell $6 Billion Bond Even as Iran Strikes
top_signals.summary: Kuwait is set to raise $6 billion from a three-part dollar bond sale on Wednesday, highlighting resilient demand for the OPEC member’s debt even as it comes under daily missile and drone attacks from Iran.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Stocks Churn Before Big-Tech Earnings as Oil Jumps: Markets Wrap
top_signals.summary: Wall Street investors bracing for the start of the megacap earnings season sent stocks wavering while worries about an escalation of the Iran war boosted oil prices.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Japan May Move to Managing Yields, Not Yen, Deutsche Bank Says
top_signals.summary: Japan may have to shift its focus from supporting the yen toward controlling government bond yields if it is to achieve its ambitious economic growth plans, according to Deutsche Bank AG.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Boeing on track for 2028 Air Force One delivery, costs rise
top_signals.summary: Boeing remains on track to deliver two new Air Force One jets in 2028, but meeting that target will require additional spending.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
top_signals.summary: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Digital Chamber sues Illinois officials over new state 0.2% crypto tax
top_signals.summary: The group alleged that the tax signed into law in June “discriminates against people who transact in digital assets“ and should be blocked from implementation and enforcement.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Prologis Wins Segro Backing for £14 Billion Takeover
top_signals.summary: Prologis Inc. is set to acquire the UK’s largest landlord Segro Plc after securing the recommendation of its board for an improved £14 billion ($18.7 billion) cash and share offer for the company.

top_signals.title: Segro board signals it would back £14bn takeover after battle with US bidder
top_signals.summary: Real estate group Prologis’s ‘best and final’ approach for London-listed rival follows previous £13.5bn bid

top_signals.title: Japan’s Power Prices Soar to 3.5-Year High
top_signals.summary: Japan’s day-ahead spot electricity prices surged on Wednesday to the highest level in three and a half years as soaring fuel costs, heat waves, and a weakening Japanese currency have combined to push power prices higher this summer. Data from the Japan Electric Power Exchange showed that nationwide day-ahead spot price surged to $0.15 (24.78 Japanese yen) per kilowatt-hour (kWh) on Wednesday, Bloomberg reports. The price has surged by 24% so far this week and hit on Wednesday the highest level since January 2023. Weather forecasts point to…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: ‘Popa’ Botnet Linked to Publicly-Traded Israeli Firm
top_signals.summary: For the past four years, a sprawling Android-based botnet called Popa has forced millions of consumer TV boxes to relay Internet traffic linked to advertising fraud, account takeovers, and mass data-scraping efforts. This week, researchers from multiple security firms concluded that the Popa botnet is linked to NetNut, a "residential proxy" provider operated by the publicly-traded Israeli firm Alarum Technologies Ltd [NASDAQ: ALAR].
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
top_signals.summary: Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport network in the Greater London area. The duo were key members of a prolific cybercrime group known as Scattered Spider, and their guilty pleas came on the first day of what was expected to be a six-week trial.
